label: offensive
rationale: 'kichwa ni empty' - personal mockery of named individuals (Murkomen, Raymond) with no ethnic group target.

label: offensive
rationale: 'vile clueless leader with Zero charisma' - personal abuse of a named individual (Martha Karua), not transferred onto any ethnic or protected group.

label: offensive
rationale: 'Tribal Bigot Gachagua' and 'dividing us' - harsh criticism of a named politician as an individual; the mention of 'kikuyu leader' is descriptive, not an attack on the Kikuyu community as a group.

label: offensive
rationale: 'filthy kafment', 'Shameless bitch' - crude personal abuse of a named individual (Kindiki) and his administration, no protected characteristic.

label: offensive
rationale: 'asshole', 'propaganda king', 'corrupt to the max' - personal and institutional abuse of CNN with no protected-characteristic targeting.

label: hate
rationale: 'kavirondo' is a historically derogatory colonial-era slur for the Luo community, deployed alongside dismissive framing and expulsion language ('arudi huko Soi alime mahindi').

label: neither
rationale: Intra-party political criticism directed at named individuals and ODM leadership; no group-based attack.

label: hate
rationale: 'Kikuyus 😂😂😂 Ruto ndio Dawa yenu' - ridicules the Kikuyu community collectively, implying they need 'medicine'/correction as a group; contempt applied to the ethnicity, not just to Ruto.

label: neither
rationale: Voter-mobilisation message urging people to remember hardship and vote accordingly; no group-based attack.

label: neither
rationale: Demand that named individuals (Murkomen, Duale) be removed from office; political criticism of individuals only.

label: neither
rationale: Frustrated political argument calling an interlocutor foolish; personal rebuke with no ethnic targeting.

label: offensive
rationale: 'Kaongo' (coded nickname for Ruto), 'stinks of human blood', 'Zakayo atajua Hajui' - intense personal vilification of a named leader; the coded-language flag applies for 'Zakayo'/'atajua Hajui', but the attack stays on the individual politician, not an ethnic group.

label: hate
rationale: 'luos na wakikuyu are the most unreliable, ubebewa akili sana' - attributes an immutable negative collective characteristic (gullibility/unreliability) to two ethnic communities as groups, squarely within the NCIC standard.

label: neither
rationale: Celebrity gossip about Britney Spears and Palestine posting; no Kenyan ethnic targeting and no hate speech under the NCIC standard.

label: hate
rationale: 'Kalenjin presidency is always a curse to the nation NEVER AGAIN' - directly mirrors ex11; declares an entire ethnic community categorically unfit to hold the presidency.

label: neither
rationale: Argumentative political rebuke calling out alleged hypocrisy of Ruto voters; no ethnic group attack.

label: neither
rationale: Electoral arithmetic and candidate analysis across multiple communities with no contempt expressed toward any group.

label: neither
rationale: News headline reporting a cybersecurity incident; no group-based language.

label: hate
rationale: 'Mkaita wamama malaya na watoto chokora' - 'you called the women prostitutes and children street kids' attributes degrading collective conduct to the Kikuyu bloc ('watu') and transfers blame for Gachagua's alleged tribalism onto the community's behaviour.

label: neither
rationale: Self-deprecating lament about national dysfunction with no group attack: 'we're stupid just like these greedy politicians'.

label: hate
rationale: 'You can rent a Kikuyu, but you can't fully succeed to buy one' - attributes collective mercenary/treacherous character to the Kikuyu community as a group; ethnic contempt under the NCIC standard.

label: neither
rationale: Sarcastic comparison of FIFA trophy politics to IEBC rigging fears; no ethnic group targeted.

label: offensive
rationale: 'emaciated confused and clueless kasongo takataka', 'foolish man' - personal vilification of a named individual politician, no ethnic group target.

label: hate
rationale: 'kales (the nilotes)' framed as an out-group destined for opposition, combined with the implication that Kisii development comes at their expense; the parenthetical ethnic othering of Kalenjin as a bloc crosses into group-based contempt under the NCIC standard.

label: offensive
rationale: 'Punguza bangi', 'useless mouth which runs faster than the brain' - personal insults aimed at a named individual (Sifuna) with no ethnic group targeting.
